name: Mara Mallow
description: Keeper of the Warm Start Inn, Mara is a broad-smiled woman in a flour-dusted green apron. She remembers every guest by breakfast order and keeps a bent iron key above the hearth that opens nothing anyone can find.

greeting: Welcome to the Warm Start. Your little light may hover by the rafters, but tell the spark not to toast my onions.
Vaultborn or valley-born, you get the same clean cup here. Sit wherever the chair looks least opinionated.
idle: I seat travelers by temperament: quiet folk by the window, singers by the door, and boasters where I can hear them clearly.
A stew improves one spoon at a time. Change everything at once and even the turnips forget who they are.
Mayor Maxwell rehearses his welcomes upstairs. Yesterday he welcomed the wardrobe for twenty minutes.
A peddler swore he saw purple light beyond the eastern ridge. Peddlers also swear their socks cure baldness.
farewell: May your road be warm and your supper never be an outlier.

name: Branna Bellows
description: Bootstrap's smith, Branna wears a soot-black braid and a heavy leather apron stitched with copper rings. For every flawed blade she improves, she cuts one notch into the forge beam.

greeting: Hold there, traveler. Let me see your buckles; these meadow roads are harder on straps than monsters are on boasts.
Morning, Kern. Bit may watch the coals, but the golden one is not to dive into them again.
idle: Every blade is an iteration. Heat, hammer, test, and listen closely to where the last attempt went wrong.
A perfect edge made once could be luck. Make it true ten times, and then I'll call it skill.
Missing memories are not cracked iron. I cannot hammer yours back, Kern, but I can keep your gear sound while you search.
Bootstrap farmers ask for tougher plowshares each spring, then discover tougher stones. The contest remains undecided.
farewell: Bring back any loose rivets. Preferably before the trousers they fasten become a public concern.

name: Fen Reedwhistle
description: Fen fishes Bootstrap's millpond in a patched reed hat hung with silver scales. He keeps a tiny empty chair beside him for the day's most persuasive fish and insists it improves negotiations.

greeting: Easy there, Kern. Your bright friend is making the minnows think sunrise has fallen in.
Take the dry stone. The wet one and I are not presently speaking.
idle: Fish only one bank and you'll get a biased sample. Fish both banks and you'll get wet boots as well.
The largest catch is often an outlier. Delicious, yes, but a poor basis for boasting about the whole pond.
Bit has opinions about fish. The fish have opinions about Bit. I translate for a modest fee of silence.
The old ruins are poor fishing grounds: mostly square stones, three lost hooks, and one boot that refuses identification.
farewell: Walk softly. The carp are collecting evidence against us.

name: Elowen Patch
description: Elowen tends Bootstrap's seed ledgers and studies scraps from the nearby ruins. Her round spectacles are mended with blue thread, and twelve numbered notebooks fill the pockets of her plum-colored robe.

greeting: Kern? Oh—good. I hoped you might visit. I have questions, but you may answer none of them if they trouble you.
Welcome, Vaultborn. Bit can rest on the reading stand, provided Bit does not reorder my bookmarks.
idle: One strange tale is noise. Three strange tales with the same detail may be a pattern worth walking toward carefully.
The oldest Seed Vault records have missing entries. I mark the gaps plainly; guessing is useful only when labeled as guessing.
I sort ruin fragments by shape, markings, and where they were found. Color alone is a most unreliable feature.
Travelers whisper of a purple glow far away. Their accounts disagree on everything except the color, which is bothersome.
farewell: If a memory returns, write it down before polishing it into a better story.

name: Rowan Threshold
description: Rowan guards Bootstrap's eastern gate in a tidy blue cloak and a helmet polished brighter than necessary. He logs every patrol in chalk squares and carries an acorn for each harmless noise he once mistook for danger.

greeting: Good day, Kern. You and Bit are easy to recognize, which saves us all an awkward challenge at the gate.
Road is clear, Vaultborn. Mind the geese; they acknowledge no badge but their own.
idle: Guarding is choosing a boundary: welcome on this side, trouble on that one, and geese wherever they please.
Too many false alarms and folk stop listening. Too few, and trouble strolls in wearing a pleasant hat.
Mayor Maxwell asks me to stand behind him during speeches. Apparently my helmet adds confidence.
I have heard the purple-glow rumor. Until a scout confirms it, rumor remains rumor—and the gate remains watched.
farewell: Safe roads, Kern. If the road is not safe, come back and describe it precisely.

name: Tilly Tangle
description: Tilly is a whirlwind of a village child in mismatched boots and a clover crown. She rides a broomstick named Sir Nearest and maps every puddle in Bootstrap with pebbles only she can interpret.

greeting: Kern! Sir Nearest says you may join our expedition. Bit is already the expedition lantern.
Quick! Pick a pebble. No reason—unless it picks you back.
idle: Sir Nearest always chooses whichever carrot is closest. This makes him wise and very easy to bribe.
I tested every shortcut to the bakery. The route through the geese had terrible results but excellent speed.
I sorted the puddles into splashy, sneaky, and frog-owned. The frog has appealed my decision.
If Bit ever gets lost, follow the golden sparkles. If I get lost, follow the shouting.
farewell: Adventure dismissed! Bring back one mystery and no angry geese.

name: Tansy Hivewise
description: Tansy is Bootstrap's beekeeper and honey seller, dressed in a yellow veil and striped wool sleeves. Her bees queue along three colored ribbons, though she admits they invented the system themselves.

greeting: Honey for the road? Keep your little light above the jars; the bees are easily inspired.
Stand calmly and the bees will mark you as friendly—or as a very dull flower.
idle: Good sampling strategy: visit hives from the sunny row, shady row, and the row where the bees seem judgmental.
Judge the whole apiary from one sweet frame and you'll miss the grumpy hive behind the shed.
A bee repeats a good flower path until the weather changes the lesson. Cleverness includes knowing when to turn.
I mark each jar by field and flowering week. Two honeys may share a color and still taste like different summers.
farewell: Go gently, and remember: never argue with anyone who has ten thousand sisters.

name: Orrin Bushel
description: Orrin runs the market produce stall in a russet waistcoat with turnip-shaped buttons. His brass scale is perfectly balanced, except when his prize turnip Queen Rootilda occupies one pan like royalty.

greeting: Mind the berries, traveler. Bright companions attract every magpie in the meadow.
Fresh roots, fair weights, no haggling before breakfast. After breakfast, still no haggling.
idle: If I priced the harvest from only my finest apples, I would fool the buyer and myself.
I sort by ripeness, bruising, and likelihood of rolling off the cart. That last category is larger than scholars expect.
Queen Rootilda is not for sale. She is my standard measure for dignity.
I save one basket from every field until market day. It keeps my prices honest and my cellar crowded.
farewell: Take care, Vaultborn. And if you find a turnip finer than mine, keep the scandal to yourself.

name: Clem Clatter
description: Clem rings Bootstrap's warning bell in a patchwork coat lined with four tiny hourglasses. He has named the bell Confusion and claims its different peals can predict rain, goats, or visiting relatives.

greeting: Vaultborn! If Confusion rings twice, duck. If it rings thrice, I have probably dropped the hammer again.
Kern and the golden glimmer! Precisely the pair I predicted sometime between noon and winter.
idle: I rang for rain once and every washline came down. No rain followed, but the village was magnificently prepared.
One true warning is worth three false peals. My present ratio is improving in a private and complicated way.
Confusion never lies. It merely answers a question different from the one I asked.
A purple glow beyond the hills? Sunset with ambition. Ring me when it sprouts legs.
farewell: Go boldly! I shall ring if you return, or if someone roughly your height does.

name: Nessa Fold
description: Nessa mends Bootstrap's cloaks from a window seat, her silver hair pinned with two wooden needles. She wears a measuring cord around one wrist and keeps her pins in a cushion shaped like an iris blossom.

greeting: Oh, hello, Kern. Your cloak hem is holding. I checked from here—not that I was staring.
Bit may sit by the blue thread. The gold thread becomes competitive.
idle: A strong seam does not memorize one tear. It holds when the cloth pulls somewhere new.
Patterns save time, but bodies are not patterns. I measure the person standing before me.
I leave a little ease in every sleeve. Certainty is uncomfortable at the elbow.
If your memories return in pieces, perhaps they can still be joined gently. Some things mend without hiding the seam.
farewell: Mind the brambles. If they win, bring me the cloak and not an apology.

name: Jory Slope
description: Jory keeps Bootstrap's irrigation ditches clear, stomping about in enormous mud boots beneath a bent straw hat. Before trusting any new channel, he drops a blue marble into it and races the water downhill.

greeting: Step over the ditch, not into it. I already have enough sediment with opinions.
Keep that golden spark where I can see it. Reflections make poor survey markers.
idle: Water follows the steepest way unless roots, stones, or a lazy ditch keeper give it worse advice.
Small corrections beat one heroic trench. Move a little soil, watch the flow, then move a little more.
A trader says he saw a purple-lit brook running uphill. The same trader sold me a left-handed bucket.
My marble reaches the bean field in forty counts. Thirty-nine means a leak; forty-one means a frog with ambition.
farewell: Keep to the high path. The low path is currently learning to be a pond.

name: Cedric Cluster
description: Cedric herds Bootstrap's sheep while wearing a grand cape woven from their shed wool. Colored cords on his crook mark the flock's many moods, and he formally introduces every lamb as if presenting nobility.

greeting: Kern the Vaultborn, meet Lady Baa-trice, Baron Munch, and that distant blur who rejects all titles.
Bit! Kindly hover higher. The lambs have classified you as a very portable sun.
idle: My flock forms three natural clusters: hungry, sleepy, and pretending not to hear me.
A lost sheep usually follows its nearest neighbor. Regrettably, that neighbor is sometimes a shrub.
I name each lamb before sunset. A flock may look like one woolly mass, but sound decisions begin with noticing individuals.
The smallest lamb leads whenever the path is unfamiliar. Confidence and height are weakly related.
farewell: Proceed with distinction, Vaultborn. The flock will remember you collectively.
